data.title: NN Group Reassures on Capital — Market Talk
data.description: NN Group's results show the reinsurer's capital position remains strong, KBW analyst Michele Ballatore says in a research note. NN group reported a solvency II ratio---a key measure of capital strength--of 194%, 9% ahead of what analysts had forecast, Ballatore says. While markets took their toll as expected, NN Group's management actions positively offset those effects, which is reflected in the 300 million-euro share buyback confirmed for next year, the analyst notes. The operating capital generation for the second half was 3% ahead of expectations, although consensus for the metric won't likely change after results, he says. "The operating capital generation disclosure by source shows a positive development at the investment return line and likely a higher than expected contribution from non-life," he says. Shares rise 6.6% to 48.61 euros. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Anglo American Writes Down Value of De Beers Diamond Unit Value by $2.9 Billion — Update
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Anglo American wrote down the value of its embattled De Beers diamond business by another $2.9 billion as it seeks to ready the division for disposal as part of the miner's efforts to simplify its portfolio.

The company said Thursday that its work to separate De Beers was well under way, but slashed the unit's valuation again, having already written down the book value of the business by $1.6 billion to $7.6 billion last year.

The write-down contributed to Anglo booking a total of $3.8 billion in net impairments for 2024, dragging it to a wider loss than analysts had expected.

The London-listed company reported a net loss of $3.07 billion for 2024 compared with the $116.9 million loss forecast by analysts, according to a Visible Alpha compiled consensus. Anglo reported a net profit of $283 million in 2023.

Underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--the company's preferred profit metric--fell to $8.46 billion from $9.96 billion, missing the $8.76 billion expected by analysts.

The on-year fall reflected a 10% decrease in average prices of its commodities, Anglo said. However, cost cutting helped deliver $1.3 billion in savings with a further $500 million expected by the end of 2025, it said.

After a failed takeover approach by rival BHP last year, Anglo has sought to simplify it portfolio to focus on copper, premium iron ore and crop nutrients.

Investors have rewarded Anglo for the strategy, with shares up 35% over the past year. In Thursday morning trade, London-listed shares were up 3.5% at 24.54 pounds.

This week it agreed to sell its nickel business to a subsidiary of China's mining company MMG for up to $500 million in cash, and has already disposed of its steelmaking coal business. These two sales will generate up to $5.3 billion in gross cash proceeds, it said.

The company also said earlier this week that the planned demerger of its platinum business was on track for a separation by June.

While Anglo said Thursday that it had made major progress with its portfolio simplification, its troubled diamond business has proved to be difficult to dispose of as cheaper manufactured gemstones rise in popularity and global economic growth remains weak.

De Beers reported an underlying earnings loss of $25 million in 2024, in-line with previous guidance from Anglo of a marginally negative result. The division booked $72 million in underlying earnings in 2023.

Anglo's copper business boosted its results, with underlying earnings rising to $3.805 billion from $3.23 billion.

However, earnings within its iron-ore business slipped to $2.655 billion from $4.01 billion as the weighted average realized price for the commodity fell by 22%, the company said.

The miner reported $800 million in total dividends in 2024, equivalent to $0.64 a share, it said.

Meanwhile, Anglo said its 50.1% owned subsidiary Anglo American Sur signed an agreement with the Chilean state-owned mining company Codelco to implement a joint plan for the two companies' adjacent copper mines of Los Bronces and Andina in Chile.

The mine plan will increase copper production at minimal additional cost and is expected to generate an extra net present value of at least $5 billion before tax over the period of the agreement, Anglo said. This will be shared equally between the partners, it said. The companies expect to enter into a definitive agreement in the second half of the year, Anglo said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Oil Edges Lower Amid U.S. Tariffs Concerns, Crude Stockpiles Build — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices ease in early trade amid concerns over U.S. trade tariffs and an industry report showing a buildup in U.S. crude inventories. Brent crude is down 0.4% to $75.74 a barrel, while WTI falls 0.3% to $71.89 a barrel. U.S. President Trump said he is considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceutical products--a move that traders fear could hurt the global economy and reduce fuel demand. Meanwhile, U.S. crude stocks rose by 3.34 million barrels last week, according to reports citing figures from the American Petroleum Institute ahead of official data later on Thursday. Crude futures settled higher in the previous trading session, supported by supply uncertainties amid disruptions to Kazakhstan's oil flows and fresh EU sanctions on Russia. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Arctic blast sends natural gas to highest since 2022, and oil ticks up on supply worries

Natural-gas futures surged Wednesday, finishing at the highest in over two years, as an Arctic blast across much of the U.S. boosted demand and also appeared to weigh on production.

Activist Elliott to Seek Phillips 66 Board Seats

With a more-than-$2.5 billion stake in Phillips 66, Elliott has pushed for a streamlining of the oil refiner and seeks more representation on the company's board.

Repsol Swings to Loss Amid Production, Energy Price Declines

Repsol reported a decline in earnings, swinging to a quarterly loss, after production and energy prices fell in the final quarter of last year

Occidental Stock Drops After Mixed Results. What's Dragging on Buffett's Favorite.

OXY stock has lost 19% over the past year. Weak crude prices are to blame.

Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash.

Galp Misses Expectations; Launches Buyback

The oil-and-gas producer launched a $262 million share buyback and said its results reflected a strong performance despite a volatile economic environment.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Nutrien, Orsted, Woodside Energy, Diamondback Energy and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

New Sanctions for Putin's 'Shadow Fleet' Are on the Horizon

The EU is ready to roll out new sanctions on vessels transporting illicit oil, but the Trump administration has yet to say if it will participate.

Clean-Energy Program Fueled by Biden Takes Deep Staff Cuts Under Trump

The number of employees in the Energy Department's loan office has been sliced by roughly 25%.

Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary

The energy company plans to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise as much as $3 billion through a listing that would be one of Japan's biggest such offerings in recent years.

data.title: Japanese Yen Could Rise Further if Inflation Data Boost Bets on Higher Rates — Market Talk
data.description: The Japanese yen hits a 10-week high against the dollar and could strengthen further if inflation data support expectations for further Bank of Japan interest-rate rises, ING's Chris Turner says in a note. The market is pricing in a strong chance of an interest-rate rise in July but not fully pricing in a 25 basis-point increase until September, according to LSEG. The yen's strength in response to these "relatively modest moves" in rates is surprising, Turner says. However, Japan's inflation data at 2330 GMT "could trigger another leg lower" for USD/JPY in the short-term, he says. Nevertheless, USD/JPY is unlikely to fall much further. USD/JPY falls to a low of 149.955, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Prioritise Self-Love and Self-Care This Year For A Better You
data.description:
SINGAPORE, Feb 20, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - Self-care has traditionally taken a backseat in our fast-paced world, yet its importance cannot be overstated. Rather than viewing external relationships as the ultimate source of fulfilment, many are prioritising personal well-being and self-care. This shift has redefined how individuals approach health, empowerment, and overall quality of life.

Despite its proven benefits - reducing stress, boosting self-esteem, and strengthening our connections with others - self-care is often neglected. A recent study found that only a third of people practice self-care daily, highlighting the need to prioritise habits that nourish the mind and body.

LAC emphasises the importance of self-care, encouraging individuals from all walks of life to nurture their well-being and invest in their health.

To support this journey of self-care, LAC (pronounced L-A-C) has thoughtfully curated a selection of wellness essentials designed to help individuals juggling packed schedules feel their best, inside and out.

LAC Hair Protect - Hair Growth Formula FullCal - Highly Assimilable Calcium Citrate Formula, and Joint Protec Flex - UC-II Collagen + CurQlife Plus Turmeric

From maintaining strong bones and joints to nourishing hair health, these supplements help individuals to maintain vitality and mobility, ensuring you stay vibrant, full of life, and ready to embrace self-love this season.

Hair health is closely tied to overall well-being, and factors like stress, poor circulation, and nutrient deficiencies can contribute to hair thinning and loss. Rooted in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), LAC Activated Hair Protect combines nine natural herbs, including He Shou Wu, to support scalp health, strengthen hair follicles, and promote natural growth.

By nourishing the liver and kidneys - key organs in TCM for maintaining healthy blood circulation - this vegetarian and halal-certified formula helps improve hair strength, thickness, and shine while reducing hair loss. Ideal for those experiencing stress, frequent hair treatments, or premature greying, LAC Activated Hair Protect supports a fuller, healthier head of hair.

As simple as it sounds, prolonged day-to-day activities, alongside growing age, often brings joint issues such as the synovial fluid and calcium depletion within the bones and joints. The combination of both LAC FullCal - Highly Assimilable Calcium Citrate and Formula Joint Protec Flex - UC-II Collagen + CurQlife Plus Turmeric provides the necessary nutrients for stronger bones and joint health.

Formulated with LAC's proprietary blend, individuals will no longer need to consume separate combinations of supplements to achieve similar results, bringing much convenience and cost-effectiveness at the same time.

By prioritising self-care, individuals can cultivate health, confidence, and overall well-being, making them better equipped to navigate life's demands. LAC encourages everyone to take a step back, recharge, and invest in their health, because true well-being starts from within.

Source: LAC Global

Copyright 2025 ACN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Anglo American CEO: Better Diamon Sales Seen in U.S., India
Prompt prices fall on demand losses, weather gets milder
data.description:
European spot power prices fell on Thursday as consumption was forecast to fall sizeably amid rising temperatures and ahead of the weekend.

Brown coal generation availability is up while hard coal and gas-fired capacity has reduced, according to an analysis by LSEG, which added that there would be lower calls on German power exports.

Meteorological data for the next two to three weeks pointed to mostly milder than normal weather, LSEG analysis also showed.

French baseload power for Friday (TRFRBD1) was at 64 euros ($66.74) per megawatt hour (MWh), down 36.6% at 0845 GMT, according to data compiled by LSEG.

German day-ahead baseload (TRDEBD1) was at 90.5 euros, down 11.7%.

German wind power output was expected to go down by 2.1 gigawatts (GW) day-on-day to reach 23.2 GW, but that in France was seen up 3.4 GW at 11.1 GW on the day-ahead.

French nuclear availability dropped by two percentage points to stand at 80% of total capacity.

Power consumption in Germany is expected to decrease by 2 GW to reach 62.2 GW on Friday, and to fall by 1.9 GW in France to 62.2 GW.

Average temperatures are expected to rise by between 1 and 5 degrees Celsius in the region, come Friday.

German year-ahead power (TRDEBYZ6) was 0.5% down at 87.3 euros/MWh while French front-year baseload (TRFRBYZ6) was untraded after a close at 65 euros.

In gas, there are indications the European Commission will work on more flexible storage targets to take the sting out of prices.

Germany's THE hub has said there is no set decision yet on timing or shape of changes to national rules.

Hintco, a subsidiary of the H2Global foundation, will hold a second auction worth around 2.5 billion euros for green hydrogen, ammonia or methanol, funded by the government of Germany and the Netherlands, and to be specified in road shows in April/May.

($1 = 0.9590 euros)

India's Just Dial climbs after Nuvama upgrade
data.description:
** Shares of Just Dial Ltd up 3.3% at 867 rupees

** Nuvama Institutional Equities upgrades Indian search engine firm to "buy" from "hold" citing "tempting" valuations

** Stock up for third-straight session

** Brokerage keeps PT unchanged at 1,140 rupees

** Says while growth challenges will persist, it likes co's focus on improving profitability

** Following Q3 results in early January, stock fell ~19% up to last close

** Over 1.7 mln shares traded, 4.3x the 30-day avg volume

** Analysts tracking JUST rate it "buy" on avg - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock up 11% this week, set to end 6-week losing run, if gains hold

Mercedes-Benz CFO: To Lower Production Capacity in Germany
SBM Offshore shares hit 14-yr highs on strong outlook
data.description:
** Shares in SBM Offshore NV soar 10%, paring earlier gains of as much as 12.3%, after it provided 2025 guidance above expectations

** SBM Offshore guides for directional EBITDA and revenue of respectively about $1.55 bln and $4.9 bln, having reported record FY numbers

** ING points to EBITDA guidance $200 mln above consensus estimates

** "Overall, the story is certainly positive, driven by the strong outlook," ING says

** The broker also points to a positive surprise on FY order book increasing by $4.8 bln, implying almost $11 bln order intake

** Degroof Petercam says the company is "on track for more value creation after a strong financial year" due to a "clear rise in backlog and good opportunities for more wins"

** The broker raises its 2025 estimates to align with the guidance

** The shares hit their highest price in about 14 years

data.title: iQIYI Kicks Off $300 Million Offering of 2030 Convertible Notes
data.description:
China's iQIYI said early Thursday it commenced a $300 million offering of convertible senior notes due 2030.

The online entertainment video services provider intends to use the net proceeds to repay and/or repurchase existing debt securities.

The notes will be convertible at the option of the holders on or after Sept. 15, 2029.

data.title: Italy's Annual Construction Output Down 0.6% in December 2024
data.description:
Italy's calendar-adjusted construction output index fell 0.6% year over year in December 2024, following a revised 3.3% increase in the prior month, Istat data showed Thursday.

On a monthly basis, Italy's seasonally adjusted construction output dropped 1.7%, against the previous 2.3% growth.

data.title: Poland Industrial Output Returns to Fall
data.description:
Poland’s industrial output fell 1% year-on-year in January 2025 from a 0.2% gain in December 2024, although below market expectations of 1.4% drop.

Output decreased for mining and quarrying (-4.8% vs 3.1% in December) and electricity, gas, steam and air-conditioning supply (-2.8% vs 2.8%).

In addition, production continued to fall for manufacturing (-0.9% vs -0.1%), while it slowed for water supply, sewerage, waste management and remediation activities (3% vs 3.8%).

On a monthly basis, industrial production rose 2.3% in January 2025, rebounding from a downwardly revised 7.9% drop in December 2024.

data.title: Naturgy's Cash Flow Paves Way for Higher Dividend — Market Talk
data.description: Naturgy Energy Group's cash-flow performance allows the company to raise its dividend target in its 2025-2027 plan, Renta4's Angel Perez Llamazares says in a research note. The Spanish utility company's results exceed Renta4's expectations on most metrics despite lower energy prices, the analyst says. Cash flow was supported by the group's networks, renewables and commercialization operations and allowed the company to maintain debt broadly stable from a year before, Renta4 says. Naturgy said it revised its dividend policy for the coming years, targeting an increase in its payout to 1.90 euros a share in 2027 from 1.60 euros a share in 2024. Shares rise 4.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Mercedes-Benz Group Plans 10% Production Cost Cuts Until 2027
data.description:
Mercedes-Benz Group said Thursday it aims to minimize its production costs by 10% until 2027.

The German automotive company said it would achieve the reduction by evaluating material costs with its suppliers and through cuts in fixed costs.
